target_scopes: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets an unprivileged user gain, buy, sell, swap, launch, migrate, claim, recover, withdraw, redeem, or redirect more BNB, quote tokens, launched tokens, LP fees, tax funds, dividend funds, salt-lock fees, or other protocol/user value than intended

Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets an unprivileged user overclaim beneficiary payouts, dividend rewards, LP-fee distributions, tax rewards, migration proceeds, or token-launch related rewards beyond valid entitlement
live_context_hint: BSC proxy and implementation slots, 1361+ BNB live proxy balance, AccessControl roles, token launch/swap/bonding-curve state, quote-token configuration, salt locks, staged tokens, token beneficiaries, DEX pool info, tax/dividend modules, external routers/migrators/lockers, recent TokenCreated/TokenBought/TokenSold/LaunchedToDEX/RoleGranted/fee events, and event-derived mapping keys
protocol_focus: Portal is a BSC token-launch and trading portal behind a transparent-style proxy. It creates/stages/commits tokens, prices bonding-curve buys and sells, handles payable swap and creation flows, migrates liquidity to DEX/locker systems, manages quote-token and tax/dividend configuration, exposes AccessControl roles, and holds a large live BNB balance on the proxy. This audit focus is only concrete fund extraction or reward extraction.
attacker_profile: unprivileged token creator, stager, buyer, seller, swapper, salt locker, token beneficiary, dividend recipient, LP-fee claimant, quote-token user, permit user, receiver contract, or external caller interacting through Portal entrypoints
core_invariants: attackers must not extract BNB, quote tokens, launched tokens, LP fees, tax funds, dividend funds, salt-lock fees, or migration proceeds beyond valid entitlement

Portal proxy balances, token reserves, circulating supply, quote-token balances, beneficiary liabilities, fee accounting, tax/dividend accounting, and DEX migration balances must reconcile after every buy, sell, swap, launch, commit, claim, recover, and migration path
bonding-curve estimates and reserve/supply updates must not allow rounding, decimal, fee, or threshold errors that mint excess tokens or return excess quote/native value
salt locks, staged-token ownership, token beneficiaries, fee exemptions, blocked spammers, and role-protected state must not be bypassable by alternate entrypoints or stale event-derived state
migration and LP-lock flows must not misdirect liquidity, fees, pool ownership, locker positions, hooks, or tax/dividend proceeds to attacker-controlled addresses
recover/burn/admin-adjacent functions matter only if an unprivileged attacker can trigger or prepare a later unauthorized value movement path without admin compromise

external router, migrator, locker, permit, tax processor, dividend, hook, and token calls must not let callbacks, non-standard token behavior, or malformed return data break Portal accounting in an attacker-profitable way
high_value_surfaces: payable token launch paths: newTokenV2/newTokenV3/newTokenV4/newTokenV5/newTokenV6/newTokenV7, stageNewTokenV5, commitNewTokenV5, lockSalt, buyOnCreation
trading paths: buy, sell, swapExactInput, swapExactInputV3, quoteExactInput, previewBuy, previewSell, previewRedeem, redeem
claim and beneficiary paths: claim, delegateClaim, setTokenBeneficiary, BeneficiaryClaimed, TaxTokenAddressesUpdated, TokenDividendTokenUpdated
migration and LP-fee paths: addV4LPLiquidity, collectV4Fees, updateTokenPoolInfo, LaunchedToDEX, V4LPFeesCollected, locker/migrator/router integrations
tax/dividend/recovery paths: recoverStuckDividend, recoverStuckTaxToken, burnStuckTaxToken, changeMarketWallet, excludeAddressFromDividends, setTokenDividendToken, updateTaxTokenAddresses
configuration and role paths: grantRole, revokeRole, renounceRole, setQuoteTokenConfiguration, setFlapFeeProfile, setFeeExemption, setBitFlags, halt, haltNewTokenV7, setSpammerBlockedBatch
packed token state and mappings: _packedTokenStates, redeemRates, lpLocks, _tokenBeneficiaries, _quoteTokenConfigurations, extensions, _dexPools, _stagedTokenStagers, salt locks, role membership
math/libraries: bonding-curve reserve/supply rounding, reserve token decimals, pool address prediction, DEX threshold and fee-profile calculations
impact_mapping: Fund extraction: attacker receives BNB, quote tokens, launched tokens, tax funds, dividend funds, LP fees, migration proceeds, salt-lock value, or claimable beneficiary value without paying the required amount or satisfying entitlement
Reward extraction: attacker overclaims beneficiary proceeds, dividend/LP-fee rewards, tax rewards, launch incentives, migration proceeds, or any repeated/delegated claim value
Insolvency with extraction: attacker creates a Portal/token/accounting mismatch then withdraws, sells, redeems, swaps, migrates, claims, or recovers more value than funded

Bad launch or migration value capture: attacker manipulates token launch, staged-token commit, salt ownership, DEX threshold, pool info, or migration configuration to capture other users' funds or protocol reserves
Unauthorized redirection: funds or rewards move to an attacker-controlled beneficiary, fee receiver, dividend token, pool, locker, router, migrator, or tax processor without valid authorization
Accounting corruption only counts if it leads to attacker-controlled over-buy, over-sell, over-swap, over-claim, over-recover, over-migration, or reward overpayment
known_rejection_memory: Reject DoS, pause, halt, spam blocking, liveness, failed launch, gas-only, griefing, and temporary lock claims unless the same reachable path transfers excess value to the attacker.
Reject admin-only role misuse, proxy-admin compromise, governance/key compromise, or maintainer configuration mistakes unless an unprivileged attacker can trigger the value extraction path.
Reject pure external router/migrator/locker/token failure unless Portal accepts attacker-controlled data or sequencing that makes Portal itself overpay or misaccount.
Reject expected bonding-curve price movement, public market competition, ordinary MEV, slippage chosen by the user, or documented tax/fee behavior unless a protected invariant is broken.
Reject stale event/key speculation unless live context or local fork tests prove the current BSC deployment has matching preconditions.
Reject source-only theories that do not connect to the live proxy address, implementation ABI, reachable entrypoint, concrete value movement, and local proof plan.
